page: Amazon Web Services c5d.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 18 physical cores and 36 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Storage is provided via a 900 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance exhibits solid compute capabilities, achieving a stress-ng single-core score of 1424.99 and a multi-core score of 33337.8. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1854.85 GB/sec, which drops to 101.84 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The CPU supports AVX and AVX-512F instructions, contributing to a Passmark CPU score of 30111.19 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10437.0.

The instance is highly efficient for workloads requiring high-frequency compute paired with fast local storage, such as high-performance web serving and database operations. However, with only 2.0 GB of RAM per core, it is less suited for memory-intensive applications. Additionally, LLM inference performance is limited, particularly for larger models, with prompt processing for a 70B model dropping to a weak 5.19 tokens/sec. This makes it best suited for batch processing, distributed analytics, ad hoc compilation, and high-throughput web servers.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 36 vCPUs (18 physical cores) running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a 900 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. The instance delivers a balanced profile for compute-heavy tasks, supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance in multi-core operations, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 33337.8 and a Passmark CPU score of 30111.19. While highly capable for high-throughput web serving and database operations, its 2.0 GB of RAM per core limits its suitability for memory-bound workloads. It is optimal for batch processing, web serving, and distributed analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 vCPUs, 72 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 900 GB local NVMe SSD, designed for high-performance computing and database workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized platform featuring high-frequency processors and fast local NVMe storage for demanding batch processing and database workloads.

72.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Optimized for high-throughput web serving and database operations
